claim_short: 2012.00406 Abrahamsen Lima Martiny Delta-points in Banach spaces generated by adequate families. Full answer to the closing question asking for a condition on an adequate family A such that h_{A,p} does not contain c0, equivalently when the canonical basis of h_{A,p} is boundedly complete. The packet proves an exact block-measure criterion: h_{A,p} contains c0 iff A admits a successive sequence of finitely...

packet_title: Full Result: Block-Measure Criterion for c0 in Adequate-Family Spaces

source_question: Source paper: Trond A. Abrahamsen, Vegard Lima, and Andre Martiny, *Delta-points in Banach spaces generated by adequate families*, arXiv:2012.00406. Location: final section, after the authors note that h_{A,p} contains an isometric copy of c0 if there is an infinite set E with |A cap E| <= 1 for all A in mathcal A. Question: "What is a natural condition on mathcal A such that h_A (or h_{A,p}) does not contain c0....
